---
title: Encrypt
description: Encrypt all cached values at rest using AES-256-GCM.
icon: Lock
---

Encrypts every cached value using AES-256-GCM via `@justwant/crypto`. Values are encrypted before being written to the adapter and decrypted on read. The adapter only ever stores ciphertext.

## Setup

```ts
import { encryptPlugin } from "@justwant/cache/plugins/encrypt";
import { deriveKey } from "@justwant/crypto/derive-key";
import { createCache } from "@justwant/cache";
import { redisAdapter } from "@justwant/cache/adapters/redis";

const key = deriveKey(process.env.CACHE_SECRET!, "salt", "cache", 32);

const cache = createCache({
  adapter: redisAdapter({ redis }),
  plugins: [encryptPlugin({ key })],
});
```

## Options

| Option | Type | Required | Description |
|--------|------|----------|-------------|
| `key` | `Uint8Array` | Yes | 32-byte encryption key |

## Deriving a key

`deriveKey` from `@justwant/crypto/derive-key` uses HKDF-SHA256 and returns a `Uint8Array`:

```ts
import { deriveKey } from "@justwant/crypto/derive-key";

// deriveKey(secret, salt, info, length)
const key = deriveKey(process.env.CACHE_SECRET!, "salt", "cache", 32);
```

The `info` parameter scopes the key — use a different value per use case (e.g. `"cache"`, `"session"`) to get distinct keys from the same secret.

## Notes

- The plugin replaces the serializer. Do not combine with `serializePlugin` — they both call `context.setSerializer`.
- `decrypt` returns `null` if the ciphertext is invalid. The plugin propagates that as a cache miss.
